title: sidalcea malviflora subsp. rostrata
intro: Sidalcea malviflora subsp. rostrata, also known as the Western marsh-mallow or the tall checker blooming marshmallow plant, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the mallow family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Sidalcea malviflora subsp. rostrata include soothing sore throats, reducing inflammation, and providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in topical applications for its soothing and healing properties.
